title: Propulsion Thermal Review Meeting — 2024-04-10
text:
# Propulsion Thermal Review Meeting — 2024-04-10

**Space:** K200  
**Meeting type:** Emergency Technical Review  
**Attendees:** Marcus Thaine (Propulsion Lead), Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE), Aisha Mbeki (Test), Tyler Moss (Firmware), Felix Herrera (Integration), Rowan Ellery (Electrical)  
**Chair:** Marcus Thaine  
**Minutes recorded by:** Felix Herrera

---

## Background

This emergency review was convened following two motor thermal events in the same week: KES-187 (fwd-port motor P-1031 thermal cutout during a 45-minute endurance run, 2 April 2024) and KES-188 (repeated ESC overcurrent on fwd-stbd motor P-1031, 8 April 2024). Both events occurred during warm-weather testing at ambient temperatures of 28–32°C. The original TorqueDrive 4012 490KV motors (P-1031) have shown a pattern of thermal issues that the thermal pads (P-1045) and standard heatsinks (P-1040 ESC mounting plate) are not adequately managing.

## Root Cause Summary

Marcus Thaine presented the thermal analysis. Key findings:

1. The P-1031 motor stator winding temperature was reaching 95–105°C during sustained hover at 28°C ambient, against a rating of 85°C maximum continuous.
2. The ESC (P-1032, ControlAir 60A) was throttling at 35°C case temperature due to its internal protection — cutting power to the motor and causing the observed overcurrent oscillation.
3. The root cause is insufficient thermal mass and poor stator-to-case conductivity in the 4012 490KV winding design. The motor was selected in 2023 without a hot-day endurance test, relying on the manufacturer's datasheet rating.

Tyler Moss confirmed the motor controller board (P-1044) thermal logging data showing temperatures up to 108°C at the motor windings.

## Options Considered

1. **Add external motor cooling (fans/ducts):** Rejected — adds ~120g per motor location, structural impact to P-1012 motor mounts.
2. **Limit operations to <25°C ambient:** Rejected — customer requirement specifies 40°C maximum ambient operating temperature.
3. **Replace with TorqueDrive 4014 420KV enhanced (P-1041):** Preferred — new winding geometry reduces stator temperature by ~18°C at same operating point. Paired with 80A ESC (P-1042) and enhanced heatsink (P-1051).

## Decision

**AGREED:** Replace all four P-1031 motors and P-1032 ESCs with P-1041 / P-1042 across the entire airframe.

- Decision maker: Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE approval required for parts changes)
- Rationale: KES-187 and KES-188 demonstrate that P-1031 cannot meet the 40°C ambient temperature requirement; customer requirement is non-negotiable
- ECR to be raised: ECR-221 (Marcus Thaine to raise by 2024-04-15)
- Qualification test: Motor qualification to be documented in DOC-445
- Motor mount (P-1012) compatibility confirmed by Tobias Wren (verbal, to be documented)

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Raise ECR-221 for motor and ESC replacement | Marcus Thaine | 2024-04-15 |
| A2 | Procure 8x P-1041 and 8x P-1042 for qualification | Marcus Thaine | 2024-04-22 |
| A3 | Run thermal qualification test per DOC-445 | Aisha Mbeki | 2024-05-30 |
| A4 | Update DRW-539 motor installation drawing | Tobias Wren | 2024-05-15 |
| A5 | Update motor controller firmware for P-1041 KV rating | Tyler Moss | 2024-05-10 |
